claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API and schema already support research/fetch style evidence with WebFetch/WebSearch marked as scope=external, while still requiring project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts. The schema, validation logic, agent prompts, tests, and doctrine all explicitly distinguish between external research evidence and project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence schema with four valid scopes: project, external, user, unknown. Field definition explicitly states: 'project facts require project-scoped evidence from code, tests, docs, config, logs, or artifacts'
evidence.why: Primary schema contract evidence showing scope distinction is built into the evidence result schema

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant restricts scope values to exactly four options: new Set(['project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown'])
evidence.why: Code evidence proving only the four defined scope values are valid

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function classifies evidence as external based on url field patterns (http/https URLs) and source field patterns (external_web, webfetch, websearch, research, external_docs)
evidence.why: Core scope classification logic showing how external research evidence is detected and distinguished from project evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope validates evidence has explicit scope field matching one of four valid values. evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator validates project-scope evidence requires a concrete file path that is not a URL
evidence.why: Validation logic proving project evidence requires concrete file locators, not URLs

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues function generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when evidence is only external/user/unknown. Generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker when project-scope evidence lacks a concrete path
evidence.why: Core validation logic showing existing-project claims are blocked without project-scoped evidence with concrete paths

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness function calls both evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each evidence result, adding blockers to readiness.check
evidence.why: Integration evidence showing PRD readiness validation enforces external vs project evidence distinction

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' confirms evidence with scope=external triggers PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker for existing-project demands
evidence.why: Test evidence proving external-only evidence cannot satisfy project fact requirements

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' confirms evidence with url field but marked as scope=project triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker because URLs cannot serve as project locators
evidence.why: Test evidence proving external URLs (from WebFetch/WebSearch) cannot be mislabeled as project evidence

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent prompt: 'If the demand explicitly asks for external research/fetch/search, actually use WebFetch/WebSearch when available and record those records as scope=external' and 'WebFetch/WebSearch are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts'
evidence.why: Agent prompt evidence showing WebFetch/WebSearch are explicitly allowed but must be marked scope=external and cannot prove project facts

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors function validates evidence records and generates errors like 'evidence[${index}] must declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown'
evidence.why: Evidence dispatch validation showing scope declarations are mandatory

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'Project facts require project-scoped evidence from target项目的 code, tests, docs, config, logs or artifacts, and must carry repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence can only come from WebFetch/WebSearch or external docs, but can only be used as background research'
evidence.why: Product doctrine evidence confirming external research cannot prove target-project facts

evidence.summary: Real Claude explorer agent result shows 14 evidence records all with explicit scope='project' declarations and concrete file paths; assumptions array properly separated from facts
evidence.why: Real execution evidence showing agents properly structure evidence with scope declarations when WebFetch/WebSearch are not needed

assumptions: WebFetch and WebSearch tools are available in the agent tooling environment when research tool profile is active
Evidence scope validation logic in router.ts correctly handles all edge cases for external URL detection and project path validation
risks: If evidence scope validation has edge cases not covered by the four main test scenarios, invalid external evidence could bypass validation
If agents ignore prompt rules about marking WebFetch/WebSearch as scope=external, the system would rely solely on post-validation blockers

result.notes: Evidence scope distinction is fully implemented and operational. Schema requires four scope values (project, external, user, unknown). Router classifies external evidence from URLs and webfetch/websearch source patterns. Project evidence requires concrete file paths, not URLs. Tests confirm external-only evidence blocks project claims and external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence. Agent prompts explicitly instruct to use WebFetch/WebSearch for external research but mark as scope=external and not use as project facts. Doctrine confirms external research is background only. Acceptance criteria met: agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence marked scope=external, and target-project facts still require project-scoped path evidence.
